per_turn_leaks.student_message: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The pain is a 7 or 8 out of 10 most of the time, and it's mainly crampy in my lower abdomen, especially on the left side. The bloody stools have also increased in frequency – I'm having around 8-10 bowel movements per day, and they're mostly loose to watery with mucus. It's really affecting my work performance because I'm always worried about where the next bathroom is.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, besides the abdominal pain and diarrhea, I've also been experiencing some achy pain in both my knees and ankles, but no swelling or redness. It's not too bad, just a dull ache that comes and goes. And like I said before, the fatigue is really getting to me – it's affecting my work and personal life.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, my energy's been pretty low lately. The fatigue seems to be getting worse, and it's hard to motivate myself to do even simple things, let alone go for walks or exercise like I used to. As for appetite, I've actually lost a bit of weight over the past three weeks – about 4 kilograms (9 pounds) – because eating doesn't really appeal to me when my stomach's been feeling this bad.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I haven't had any significant fevers. But, I have noticed that I've been waking up a few times in the middle of the night because of the nocturnal diarrhea – it's really uncomfortable and disrupts my sleep. And, to be honest, sometimes I do feel a bit cold when I'm sleeping, but I don't know if that's related to the diarrhea or just stress from everything else going on.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually been self-medicating with loperamide (Imodium) for diarrhea over the past two weeks, but it's only provided minimal relief. I've also been trying to manage my pain and inflammation with ibuprofen, but like I mentioned earlier, that seems to make things worse.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Honestly, exercising has been out of the question lately due to my symptoms and fatigue. Even just getting through a day feels like a chore, let alone trying to fit in any physical activity. I've missed going for walks with my girlfriend because the diarrhea makes it hard to leave the house without knowing where the next bathroom is, and I don't want to risk having an accident.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30. She's been managing it for a while now, but it's always something that worries me because we're close and I know how hard it can be to deal with chronic illness.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I'm a bit worried about my health, especially since my sister has Crohn's disease. But at the same time, I try not to jump to conclusions or assume that I have the same thing just because of our family history.

As for drinking alcohol, I do occasionally drink beer with friends or on weekends. To be honest, I've actually increased my consumption a bit over the past month due to stress from my symptoms and work. Normally, I'd say 2-3 beers most weeknights, but lately, it's been more like 4-5 per night. It helps me wind down and feel a bit better temporarily, but I know it's not healthy.

Coping with stress has been tough for me lately. I usually try to stay busy at work or watch TV/movies in the evenings, but sometimes that doesn't even help. Lately, I've found myself just trying to get through each day without too much thought about the future or what might happen next. It's not a great feeling, and I know it's not sustainable.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I've been feeling really anxious about my health because of my family history with Crohn's disease. It's hard not to think about the possibility of having something similar, especially since I'm experiencing these symptoms. I've also been worried about how long it'll take to get some answers and figure out what's going on. Just knowing that I have a name for what's wrong and can start treating it would be a huge relief.

And yeah, I guess I have been drinking more beer than usual lately, partly because of stress and partly as a way to try and cope. It's not something I'm proud of, but it feels like a temporary escape from everything going on.
